summary: Iteration 41 - Frontend-only validation of 7 usability improvements (A1, A2, OS1, OS2, I1, I2, E1) for MANUTRIX OMNI CMMS. All features verified end-to-end via Playwright with admin login. Login (admin@manutrix.com/admin123) succeeds, dashboard renders, all 47 ativos show dynamic status badges (Operacional/Em Manutenção/Parado), 3 ativos display OS counter badges (e.g. '20 OS', '2 OS', '1 OS'), OS Kanban shows search input + 5 priority filter buttons (Todas, Emerg., Alta, Média, Baixa) - search 'XYZNOMATCH123' correctly filters cards from 30 to 0, priority filter click toggles active state, Inspeções shows 5 status filter buttons with live counters (Pendentes(5), Em Andamento(3), Concluídas(4), Com Pendências(3)) + area dropdown with 3 sectors (PLANTA-01/02/03), Estoque shows 27 expand buttons - clicking expands inline movimentações block showing 'Últimas Movimentações' with Saída entries linked to OS, collapse works. No regressions observed - dashboard loads with KPIs (98.4% disp, 23 backlog, 20 OS abertas), sidebar nav intact.

issues: Recharts emits warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on initial render — cosmetic only, charts render correctly after layout settles. Consider adding minWidth/minHeight to ResponsiveContainer wrappers.

critical_code_review_comments: App.js:2368/2372 - data-testid uses ativo.tag (e.g. ativo-status-AV-01). Observed 3 badges sharing the same testid 'ativo-status-AV-01' indicating duplicate TAGs in seed data across plants. While the runtime behavior is correct, duplicate testids reduce test selector reliability. Consider using ativo.id (UUID) for data-testid to guarantee uniqueness, keeping tag as a visible label only.
App.js:3923 - insp-filter-status counters use inspecoes.filter(...).length over the unfiltered list, which is correct (counts should reflect totals, not filtered subset). Good.
App.js:3934 - insp-filter-area dropdown options use sector.id as value — good, ensures stable filtering even if sector names change.
App.js:3140 - os-clear-filters button only appears when (searchOS || filterPriority) — good UX. Same pattern at 3937 for inspections.
App.js:3782 - movs-{item.id} block conditionally renders only when expandedItem === item.id, so test selectors should expect element absence on collapse (verified).
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Used existing org data (47 ativos, 30+ OS, 15+ inspeções, 27 estoque items, 3 sectors).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 7 frontend improvements verified working with admin credentials at https://procure-manutrix.preview.emergentagent.com. Data-testids in use: ativo-status-{tag}, ativo-os-count-{tag} (NOTE: tag-based, may duplicate), os-search-input, os-filter-priority-{all|emergencia|alta|media|baixa}, insp-filter-status-{all|pendente|em_andamento|concluida|com_pendencias}, insp-filter-area, expand-estoque-{id}, movs-{id}. To re-test, login as admin then visit /ativos, /os, /inspecoes, /estoque. No backend changes were made — backend testing intentionally skipped.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. All 7 usability features (A1, A2, OS1, OS2, I1, I2, E1) implemented correctly and functional.
